title: Hemoptysis documentation

presenting_history.text: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped cough or sputum, chest-pain or breathlessness, systemic-symptom, smoking, and prior-episode context.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

red_flags.text: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, systemic symptoms, smoking, and clinician-assessed safety fields as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-decided referral and safety documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed chest radiography, blood, and sputum fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

assessment.text: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped haemoptysis identity and aliases with recommendation-specific age and smoking qualifiers preserved.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

source_limitations: No exact source was accepted for haemoptysis amount or colour, massive-haemoptysis thresholds, chest trauma, anticoagulation risk or reversal, TB-negative summary wording, or fixed follow-up.
Known malignancy risk, broad vitals and examination fields, CT chest, coagulation testing, generic clinician plan, PRN wording, and the combined structured history remain unsupported.
Bleeding severity, airway risk, diagnosis, investigation selection, emergency disposition, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
